raw_consumer_statement: American Express violated the Fair Credit Billing Act. This law grants the right to dispute a charge with a card issuer and to withhold payment on a disputed charge until an investigation is complete. On XX/XX/2024, American Express reopened my fraud claim after I obtained new evidence independently. However, they continued to charge me interest on the disputed amounts during the two-month investigation. During phone calls with the bank, they requested that I continue making payments for the fraudulent charges.
